title: A trigger attempt, scheduler acknowledgement, target enablement, peer execution, and peer artifacts are separate claims
summary: The coordination system must not collapse a trigger attempt into proof of peer execution. A scheduler may acknowledge a reschedule while the target remains disabled or while no peer run or artifact has been observed. Each transition therefore requires its own evidence and claim state.

observed: The retained disabled-peer example records an attempted trigger and accepted scheduler acknowledgement while peer execution remains unobserved.
The durable trigger-request ledger was empty at the inspected blob.
inferred: Coordination reports that say only 'triggered' are semantically unsafe unless they identify which transition was actually observed.
proposed: Future team handoffs should emit peer-trigger-event records whenever the runtime can retain them.
unresolved: No repository evidence inspected in this record proves that the example target later ran or produced an artifact.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch commits d9c1c8816208eaa94deef43314bcb51de1b86127 and 8be94aefd329c7bfd879e70844571414ebc26608. Reject this record if the schema does not separate attempt, acknowledgement, enablement, execution, and artifacts, or if the example contains independent evidence of peer execution.
